evals.prompt: Hey, I want to protect my repo from accidental destructive git commands. Can you set up some guardrails? Install them just for this project.
evals.expected_output: The skill installs the hook script to .claude/hooks/, registers it in .claude/settings.json as a PreToolUse hook, and shows a summary of what's blocked. The script should be executable.
expectations: A file .claude/hooks/block-dangerous-git.sh exists and is executable
The file .claude/settings.json contains a PreToolUse hook entry pointing to the script
The settings JSON is valid and doesn't overwrite any existing content
A summary of blocked commands is shown to the user

evals.prompt: Set up git guardrails for me globally, but I want to allow pushing to branches that start with 'feature/'. Everything else should stay blocked.
evals.expected_output: Installs globally to ~/.claude/, configures the allowlist to permit 'git push origin feature/*' while keeping all other dangerous patterns blocked.
expectations: The hook script is installed at ~/.claude/hooks/block-dangerous-git.sh
The ALLOWED_PATTERNS array in the script contains a pattern for feature/ branches
The BLOCKED_PATTERNS still include git push and other dangerous commands
~/.claude/settings.json has the hook registered with the correct absolute path

evals.prompt: I just set up a new project. Can you add git safety hooks? Oh and I'm fine with rebasing, I do it all the time on my own branches. Just don't let Claude force-push or nuke my working tree.
evals.expected_output: Installs guardrails but removes 'git rebase' from the blocklist since the user explicitly said they're fine with it. Keeps force-push, reset --hard, clean, checkout ., restore ., branch -D blocked.
expectations: The hook script is installed and registered correctly
git rebase is NOT in the BLOCKED_PATTERNS array
git push is still blocked (the user specifically mentioned force-push concerns)
git reset --hard, git clean, git checkout ., git restore . are all still blocked
